children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an epidemiology center in each Service area to carry out the functions described in subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), any new center established after , may be operated under a grant authorized by subsection (d).March 23, 2010

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: Funding provided in a grant described in subparagraph (A) shall not be divisible.

children.heading: Funds not divisible
children.label: 3 Funds not divisible
children.text: An epidemiology center established under this subsection shall be subject to the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.), but the funds for the center shall not be divisible.25 U.S.C. 4501See References in Text note below.1

collect data relating to, and monitor progress made toward meeting, each of the health status objectives of the Service, the Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and urban Indian organizations in the Service area;
evaluate existing delivery systems, data systems, and other systems that impact the improvement of Indian health;

assist Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and urban Indian organizations in identifying highest-priority health status objectives and the services needed to achieve those objectives, based on epidemiological data;
make recommendations for the targeting of services needed by the populations served;
make recommendations to improve health care delivery systems for Indians and urban Indians;

provide requested technical assistance to Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and urban Indian organizations in the development of local health service priorities and incidence and prevalence rates of disease and other illness in the community; and
provide disease surveillance and assist Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and urban Indian communities to promote public health.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: c Technical assistance
children.text: The Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention shall provide technical assistance to the centers in carrying out this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to Indian tribes, tribal organizations, Indian organizations, and eligible intertribal consortia to conduct epidemiological studies of Indian communities.

children.label: A
children.text: incorporated for the primary purpose of improving Indian health; and

children.label: B
children.text: representative of the Indian tribes or urban Indian communities residing in the area in which the intertribal consortium is located.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 3 Applications
children.text: An application for a grant under this subsection shall be submitted in such manner and at such time as the Secretary shall prescribe.

children.label: A
children.text: demonstrate the technical, administrative, and financial expertise necessary to carry out the functions described in paragraph (5);

children.label: B
children.text: consult and cooperate with providers of related health and social services in order to avoid duplication of existing services; and

children.label: C
children.text: demonstrate cooperation from Indian tribes or urban Indian organizations in the area to be served.

children.label: A
children.text: to carry out the functions described in subsection (b);

children.label: B
children.text: to provide information to, and consult with, tribal leaders, urban Indian community leaders, and related health staff regarding health care and health service management issues; and

children.label: C
children.text: in collaboration with Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and urban Indian organizations, to provide to the Service information regarding ways to improve the health status of Indians.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An epidemiology center operated by a grantee pursuant to a grant awarded under subsection (d) shall be treated as a public health authority (as defined in section 164.501 of title 45, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation)) for purposes of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (; ).Public Law 104–191110 Stat. 1936

children.heading: Access to information
children.label: 2 Access to information
children.text: The Secretary shall grant to each epidemiology center described in paragraph (1) access to use of the data, data sets, monitoring systems, delivery systems, and other protected health information in the possession of the Secretary.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 3 Requirement
children.text: The activities of an epidemiology center described in paragraph (1) shall be for the purposes of research and for preventing and controlling disease, injury, or disability (as those activities are described in section 164.512 of title 45, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation)), for purposes of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (; ).Public Law 104–191110 Stat. 1936
